name: Dakota Staton
abstract: Dakota Staton (June 3, 1930 – April 10, 2007) was an American jazz vocalist who found international acclaim with the 1957 No. 4 hit "The Late, Late Show".

concerts.label: Dakota Staton, Singer, the Ahmad Jamal Trio, and the Ted Heath Orchestra
Midnight Performance: Dakota Staton, Singer, the Ahmad Jamal Trio, and the Ted Heath Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: I got a call from Dakota Staton. And I went to New York, rehearsed with her, and then we... I think we worked maybe a club or so... Dakota seemed to be the kind of a person who would pay a lot of compliments about the way you were playing one night, and the next night there would be something wrong

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: So he figures out a contract escalating the money and so first they got -- I think Dave is just getting his foot in the door, but he gets Dakota Staton. So he has -- he says, 'I want you to make a record date with Dakota.' ... So we did, we went down and made the record, Late, Late Show

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: I started – Dakota Staton was through – I met Dakota Staton through Dave Cavanaugh... We came up on an agreement... I worked with her and put together a background – a group of musicians to work with her.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: When we were recording the Late Late Show, we did a thing with Dakota Staton.

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: I had been at a band camp that summer... We’re going to be sharing the evening with Dakota Staton, a singer who I’d heard on record... Then here came Miles’ band... So we played, and then Dakota Staton’s band came on
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: I remember the club used to go dark, and you’d only hear my voice saying, “Ladies and gentlemen, Annie’s Room is proud to present Dakota Staton.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
